LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COMPLAINT AND THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DISCLOSE THAT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOWNTOWN PARKING CORPORATION WAS FIRST SUED MORE THAN TWO YEARS AFTER THE INCIDENT COMPLAINED OF. BECAUSE IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO ALLEGE FACTS PRECLUDING A SUCCESSFUL STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE BY THIS DEFENDANT, PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLEGING WHY HER CLAIM AGAINST THIS DEFENDANT IS NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963; DEPT. 302 (302/HK)

THE MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 22, 2016 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)

ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOWNTOWN PARKING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOWNTOWN PARKING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE CAUSE IS ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY BOTH SIDES PRESENT. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER (FILED 4/21/15) AND HER COUNSEL'S DECLARATION DEMONSTRATE GENUINE IGNORANCE OF DEFENDANT'S IDENTITY AT THE TIME THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT WAS FILED. THUS, THE CCP 474 DOE AMENDMENT WAS PROPER AND IT RELATES BACK TO THE DATE THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT WAS FILED. I FURTHER NOTE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS REASONABLY CONFUSED REGARDING DEFENDANT'S IDENTITY AS SHE DID NOT RECEIVE THE GOVERNMENT CLAIM DENIAL LETTER AND DEFENDANT'S NAME INCLUDES IN IT "CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO." I GRANT DEFENDANT'S JUDICIAL NOTICE REQUEST. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-03-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-18-2016 Continued To Jan-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Mallory L. Homewood of Bowles & Verna LLP (925-935-3300) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Megan Symonds of Law Offices of Santana Tcheng Vierra & Symonds (415-777-1308 ext. 16079) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Downtown Parking Corporation

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides present. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOWNTOWN PARKING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff's declaration in opposition to defendant's demurrer (filed 4/21/15) and her counsel's declaration demonstrate genuine ignorance of defendant's identity at the time the original complaint was filed. Thus, the CCP 474 Doe amendment was proper and it relates back to the date the original complaint was filed. I further note that plaintiff was reasonably confused regarding defendant's identity as she did not receive the government claim denial letter and defendant's name includes in it "City of San Francisco." I grant defendant's judicial notice request. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2016 Set For Trial On Sep-12-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JONATHAN W. LEE, ESQ. of Bowies & Verna, LLP, Tel No. 925-935-3300 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MICHAEL K. TCHENG, ESQ. of Law Offices of Santana, Tcheng, Vierra & Symonds, Tel No. 415-276-0705 appearing for Defendant City of San Francisco Downtown Parking Corporation.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOWNTOWN PARKING CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. In substance, although perhaps not in proper form, plaintiff has sought to avail herself of the "Doe procedures" adding a named party that relates back to the filing of the lawsuit for statute of limitations purposes. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts, which are not necessarily contradicted by anything that is judicially noticeable, to enable her to do so. Any non-compliance by plaintiff with usual Doe procedures is both non-substantive and harmless to defendant. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOWNTOWN PARKING CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to February 5, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 to give the moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 22, 2016 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-11-2015 Continued To Aug-05-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOWNTOWN PARKING CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The complaint and the first amended complaint disclose that City of San Francisco Downtown Parking Corporation was first sued more than two years after the incident complained of. Because it appears that plaintiff may be able to allege facts precluding a successful statute of limitations defense by this defendant, plaintiff is granted leave to file a second amended complaint alleging why her claim against this defendant is not barred by the statute of limitations. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963 (302/HK)
